title: Software Engineer 2
description: We are seeking passionate and driven individuals with a strong foundation in computer science to join our team. In this role, you'll have the opportunity to design, build, and test software that enhances MongoDB products while solving complex technical challenges. You'll collaborate with cross\-functional teams, balance innovation with delivery, and contribute to features that empower our global community of users. Join us in a supportive and inclusive environment that values your unique perspective and fuels your growth. We are looking to speak to candidates who are based in Dublin for our hybrid working model. ### **What we are looking for in you** * In the process of receiving or having received a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field * Recent graduates (including Summer 2026\) or professionals with up to 18 months of experience. * Have a foundation in computer science theory, with competencies in data structures, algorithms, and software design * Proficient in one of our commonly used languages which include C\+\+, Go, Ruby, Python, Java, Javascript, Node.js, or comparable high level programming languages * Experience writing and testing software whether as part of an internship, in a personal project, in a coding competition, or for research ### **What you'll find at MongoDB** * Writing and testing software in commonly used high level programming languages, such as C\+\+, Go, Ruby, Python, Java, Javascript, Node.js * Identifying, designing, testing, and supporting new features in MongoDB products, particularly utilizing competencies in data structures, algorithms, and software design * Leading software projects that help users maintain and understand their MongoDB deployments * Solving problems with context in mind, balancing the needs of the team with the desire to get to the bottom of issues * Maintaining a balance between delivering software and performing research and development * Working closely with cross functional teams to create end user features that deliver quality software to our clients * A fun community of fellow New Grads to attend social events, hangouts, and games nights * Membership within our Employee Resource Groups where you can gain mentorship and learning opportunities from employees with a shared background or common identity * A workplace culture where you are encouraged to bring your whole self to work \- we believe that Embracing the Power of Differences is the best way to broaden our own perspectives and foster innovation ### **About Us** MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB's unified database platform—the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market—helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud\-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi\-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure. With offices worldwide and nearly 60,000 customers—including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI\-native startups—relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we're powering the next era of software. Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It's what makes us MongoDB. To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we're committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees' wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it's like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world! MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your Early Talent Recruiter. MongoDB is an equal opportunities employer. *Req ID: 1273361981*

title: Software engineer, intern
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. In this six\-month internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Build software that matters.** Take real ownership from idea to production, creating systems used by millions and evolving them into products at scale. * **Amplify your impact with AI.** Integrate the most powerful AI tools directly into your development workflow—design, implementation, testing, and documentation—to move faster while maintaining high standards for correctness, reliability, and maintainability. * **Master your toolkit.** Work across diverse stacks with end\-to\-end ownership, choosing the right technologies for each challenge. From monoliths to microservices, gRPC to REST, Kubernetes to Docker, Python to Rust—you’ll apply technologies thoughtfully, focusing on depth and purpose rather than trends. * **Simplify relentlessly.** Question every layer of complexity. Improve architectures, pipelines, and codebases to build systems that are simpler, more scalable, and easier to maintain. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. * **A flexible start date and part\-time options.** You don't need to wait until graduation to apply. We offer flexible start dates and the possibility to begin part\-time, transitioning to full\-time as you complete your degree. Many Spooners joined before graduating and progressively took on greater responsibility, with arrangements that allowed them to do so without compromising their education. **Commitment \& contract** Internship. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland), London (UK) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Sous Chef Drogheda
description: **Job Title: Sous Chef** ------------------------ **Location:** Social Restaurant, Drogheda, Co. Louth ### **Overview** Social Restaurant is seeking an experienced and motivated Sous Chef to support the Head Chef in delivering high\-quality, contemporary food in a busy and vibrant restaurant environment. This role is ideal for a strong, hands\-on chef who thrives in service, leads by example, and is passionate about consistency, teamwork, and fresh produce. ### **Key Responsibilities** **Kitchen Leadership** * Support the Head Chef in the day\-to\-day running of the kitchen * Lead kitchen operations during service, ensuring smooth, efficient delivery * Step in to manage the kitchen in the Head Chef's absence **Food Quality \& Standards** * Ensure all dishes are prepared and presented to a consistently high standard * Assist with menu development, specials, and seasonal changes * Maintain strong attention to detail across all sections **Team Support \& Development** * Supervise, train, and motivate junior kitchen staff * Help foster a positive, professional, and respectful kitchen culture * Lead by example in attitude, work ethic, and standards **Operations \& Compliance** * Assist with stock control, ordering, and minimising waste * Ensure full compliance with HACCP, food safety, and health \& safety standards * Maintain cleanliness and organisation throughout the kitchen ### **Candidate Profile** * Proven experience as a Sous Chef or strong Senior Chef de Partie * Experience in a fast\-paced, quality\-driven restaurant environment * Strong organisational and communication skills * Ability to work calmly under pressure and during busy services * A genuine passion for food, learning, and teamwork ### **What We Offer** * Competitive salary, based on experience * A supportive and professional kitchen environment * Opportunities for development and progression * The chance to be part of a well\-established and respected local restaurant

title: Strategic Account Executive 6 (German)
description: Wrike is the most powerful work management platform. Built for teams and organizations looking to collaborate, create, and exceed every day, Wrike brings everyone and all work into a single place to remove complexity, increase productivity, and free people up to focus on their most purposeful work. **Our vision:** A world where everyone is free to focus on their most purposeful work, together. ### **About the Role:** As an Strategic Account Executive 6 at Wrike, you will play a critical role in landing \& expanding key enterprise accounts. You'll own the full sales cycle with new logos, expansion, engaging at C\-suite/executive level, partnering with cross\-functional teams, and demonstrating Wrike's impact on enterprise transformation and ROI. You'll thrive in a fast\-paced, high\-growth environment where your consultative approach and tenacity will directly unlock new business and deepen Wrike's brand as a leader in the market. If you're a collaborative professional who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and enjoys connecting technology solutions to real business challenges, we want to hear from you! ### **Your Impact:** * Develop, manage, and close complex sales cycles; prospecting, leading discovery, presenting business cases, negotiating contracts, and landing new logos at the enterprise level (10,000\+ employees). * Deliver compelling presentations and business cases to executive audiences, demonstrating strategic thinking, business acumen, and exceptional consultative communication skills. * Provide highly accurate and insight\-driven monthly, quarterly, and annual pipeline forecasts, and maintain meticulous CRM (Salesforce) hygiene. * Serve as an expert consultant on digital and operational transformation; drive opportunities to upsell, cross\-sell, and expand engagement within enterprise accounts. * Proactively lead and coordinate cross\-functional account teams—including Marketing, Sales Development, Solution Consultants, Legal, Security, and Implementation—to deliver a world\-class enterprise client experience. * Skillfully navigate complex legal, procurement, compliance, and security processes in collaboration with internal teams and enterprise client stakeholders. * Stay at the forefront of SaaS trends and industry best practices to be a valued advisor to your clients and the broader Wrike team. * Guide enterprise buyers through organizational change management and adoption planning, ensuring alignment, education, and readiness across large buying groups. * Ensure a seamless and comprehensive handoff to Customer Success and Implementation teams, clearly documenting goals, expectations, and key learnings for ongoing client engagement. * Regularly capture and communicate enterprise customer insights, competitive intelligence, and market feedback to Product, Sales, and Marketing leadership for continuous improvement. * Mentor and support development of junior account executives, sharing best practices and contributing to team enablement initiatives. ### **Your Qualifications:** * Fluent in English \& German (verbal and written) * 5\+ years of successful full\-cycle SaaS sales experience, including enterprise\-level new business acquisition (large deal sizes, multiple stakeholders) * Demonstrated success executing complex deals with large enterprise organizations and C\-suite stakeholders * Skilled in delivering consultative, solution\-based sales cycles using methodologies like MEDDIC, Challenger, or Question\-Based Selling * Outstanding executive presence, business acumen, and ability to translate technical value into compelling ROI/value stories tailored to customer needs * Proven ability to manage multiple sales processes and large pipelines, with consistent attainment or overachievement of quotas * Strong written, verbal, and presentation skills; comfortable leading high\-stakes executive meetings and product demos * Experience managing legal, procurement, and information security processes for large enterprise agreements * Proficiency with Salesforce and the modern SaaS sales tech stack ### **Standout Qualities:** Experience in work management, project management, or a track record of selling to our ideal customer profiles (ICPs) in Marketing, Professional Services, Manufacturing or Engineering. ### **Team Dynamics:** You'll join Wrike's high\-performing New Business team, where your primary focus will be acquiring new enterprise customers and driving revenue growth. As a key member of our enterprise sales organization, you'll play a critical role in expanding Wrike's presence by landing new logos and expanding our existing business , shaping our go\-to\-market strategy, and fueling our continued growth in the enterprise segment. ### **Our Work Style:** * Collaborative Culture: Work alongside ambitious, supportive colleagues who celebrate wins together * Comprehensive Training: Structured onboarding and ongoing sales enablement to help you master our product and refine your skills * Work Flexibility: Options for remote work with the support you need to succeed from your home or the office * Competitive Compensation: Base salary plus uncapped commission structure that rewards performance ### **Why Join Wrike?** * 25 calendar days of paid vacation * Parental Leave: 26 Weeks Maternity / 4 Week Paternity * 2 Volunteer Days * Bike\-to\-Work Scheme * Health Insurance (Employees \+ Dependents) * Life Insurance * Income Protection * Pension Scheme Your recruitment buddy will be Aziza Talhi, Senior Recruiter. \#LI\-AT1 ### **Who Is Wrike and Our Culture** We're a team of innovators and creators who solve the complex work problems of today and tomorrow. **Hybrid work mode** Wrike promotes a hybrid model for team members near our office hubs in San Diego, Prague, Dublin, Nicosia, and Tallinn, with 2–3 in\-office days per week to foster collaboration and teamwork. This work mode supports our culture of collaboration and solving problems fast to deliver business outcomes and win together. #### **Our persona** **Smart:** We love what we do, and we're great at it because this is our domain. Our combined knowledge in this space is unmatched. **Dedicated:** We get up every day focused on helping our customers win. We're committed to helping our teammates win, too! **Approachable:** We're friendly, easy to get along with, considerate, and helpful. #### **Our culture and Values** **Customer\-Focused** **We care about our customers.** We understand the customer journey, experience, and value derived from Wrike. Decision\-making and action\-taking are done with the customer in mind. **Collaborative** **We work as one and win together,** each bringing unique strengths that contribute to diversity of thought for better outcomes. Leveraging our own work management platform, we foster an environment of creative collaboration and shared achievement. **Creative** **We strive to succeed through continuous innovation.** It's our pursuit of novel concepts that helped us create a market category. We continue to cultivate a workplace that fosters creative thinking as a means of transcending conventional boundaries and empowers us to break new ground to deliver extraordinary work management solutions. **Committed** **We believe in ownership at all levels of the organization,** by owning workflows from start to finish. Each member of our team is an integral part of this commitment, establishing work as a platform for personal growth and transformation, as well as collective success and growth. Check out our LinkedIn Life Page, Company culture page, Instagram, Wrike Engineering Team, Medium, Meetup.com, Youtube for a feel for what life is like at Wrike.

title: Stáž sa Turasóireacht
description: **ABOUT US** Indie Campers, the go\-to campervan Marketplace, is on a mission of making road trips available to everyone. Thanks to its digital approach and customer\-centric orientation, Indie Campers has developed a strong booking experience and high\-quality road trips at affordable prices. With over one million nights rented through our travel platform, we have welcomed more than 300,000 travelers from 169 countries. We offer a comprehensive and expanding array of road trip possibilities: short\-term RV rentals, long\-term campervan subscriptions, and the possibility of buying one of our vehicles available for sale. Rooted in a deep technological focus, our challenges are both exciting and demanding and require top talent and motivation to be successfully pursued. We are growing our team and looking for those interested in pursuing this dream with us and joining an ever\-developing journey. **THE ROLE** We're on the lookout for passionate students or recent graduates who want to dive into the exciting world of RV and campervan rentals and gain hands\-on tourism experience at our **Dublin depot** at **Clonswords Yard Ballyboughal Co Dublin A41F308, Ireland.** As our **Tourism \& Operations Intern**, you'll be the friendly face of our brand \- welcoming guests, setting them up for road trips of a lifetime, and ensuring their journey ends just as wonderfully as it began. If you thrive on meeting new people, love a challenge, and enjoy the buzz of a fast\-paced environment, your next adventure starts here! **WHAT WILL YOU WORK ON?** **Front\-office activities:** * **Customer service and sales:** Take care of check\-ins and check\-outs, register guests, process payments, explain campervan functionalities, terms and conditions, upsell road trip extras. **Back\-office activities:** * **Operations handling:** Align the reservations with the available campervans, verify and prepare campers and road trip extras and kits. * **Quality control:** Maintain the campervans in top condition, identify and act on possible maintenance or repair needs. * **Support activities:** Support the team in monitoring the existing stock, coordinating the fleet of campervans, troubleshooting operational issues. **WHO ARE WE LOOKING FOR?** * You are a student or recent graduate in Hospitality, Tourism, International Studies, Business Administration or a related field looking for an internship; * You are fluent in English, have a work permit (if applicable), and avalid driver's licence (for both manual and automatic vehicles); * You like international environments and meeting new cultures; * You possess a natural talent for connecting with people and have a strong customer service orientation; * You have a problem\-solving mindset, thriving to create solutions and feeling comfortable with getting your hands dirty. **THE INDIE COMMITMENT** * All internships are compensated; * Online and on\-the\-job training to develop relevant skills to your growth: guest relations, customer service, problem\-solving, communication, team spirit, organization, time management, fleet coordination, supplies management and logistics; * International opportunities and possibility of integration in Indie Campers after the internship. ***Are you ready to Go Indie?***

title: Tax Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Tax Analyst – Advanced Leadership Role **Sector:** Manufacturing **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid) **Salary:** €70,000 \+ 10% bonus \& benefits **Our Client** Our client is a well\-established, private equity backed international manufacturing business with a strong global presence. Operating in a fast\-paced and high\-performance environment, the organisation continues to grow through strategic investment and expansion. The group finance function, based in Dublin, plays a central role in supporting international operations and driving commercial decision\-making across the business. **Why should you apply?** This is a standout opportunity for a recently qualified Big 4 professional to step into an advanced leadership role early in their career. Unlike many industry roles which can be narrow in scope, this position offers genuinely broad exposure across tax, with involvement in both technical and commercial aspects of the business. Working directly with a highly regarded Head of Tax, you will benefit from strong mentorship and hands\-on development. The environment is demanding and fast\-paced, but it offers a steep learning curve and clear progression opportunities — evidenced by the previous hire being promoted internally. This role is ideally suited to someone ambitious who wants to accelerate their career and gain exposure well beyond a typical Tax Analyst position. **Who should apply?** This role will suit a Big 4 trained tax or accounting professional looking to make their first move into industry and take on a broader, more commercially focused role. * Qualified accountant or tax professional * 0–3 years’ post\-qualified experience (PQE), ideally from a Big 4 or large practice environment * Strong grounding in tax compliance and/or advisory * Keen to move into a broader, more commercially focused role * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ambitious, driven and comfortable in a high\-performance environment **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting directly to the Head of Tax and working closely with two experienced managers within the group finance function, this role offers significant exposure across the organisation and will involve: * **Tax Compliance** – Support the preparation and coordination of corporate tax filings across multiple jurisdictions * **Transfer Pricing** – Assist with transfer pricing documentation and intercompany arrangements * **Tax Advisory** – Provide guidance and support to finance teams on a variety of tax matters * **Legislation Monitoring** – Monitor changes in tax legislation and assess the impact on the business * **Audit \& Revenue Engagement** – Support tax audits and liaise with external advisors and tax authorities * **Tax Planning \& Projects** – Contribute to tax planning initiatives and support strategic projects including business expansion and transactions * **Tax Provisioning** – Assist in the preparation and analysis of tax provisions in line with accounting standards * **Stakeholder Collaboration** – Work closely with international finance teams to ensure compliance and best practice **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Frank Bailey who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200

title: Test Automation Engineer
description: **Test Automation Engineer** ---------------------------- Do you want to join an exciting, growing team that wants to hear your ideas? A team that wants you to be up to date on the latest technologies. Working in BearingPoint you will be part of a team that is passionate about building innovative, high\-quality software for its clients. You will be working in agile teams with all the support you need. BearingPoint is growing quickly, and we are currently hiring junior automation testers to join our QA team. **What skills and experience should you have?** ----------------------------------------------- * Passionate about technology, testing and delivering top quality software products. * At least 5 years' experience in test automation. * Practical experience of test automation including tooling selection and test framework design. * Hands\-on technical experience of building both API and UI test automation frameworks in a range of languages and technologies such as jUnit, Selenium, Serenity BDD (Cucumber, Rest Assured) Robot Framework and JMeter. * Implementation, maintenance and execution of automated test suites to ensure thorough testing of requirements in a fast\-paced Agile environment with an emphasis on BDD and BDD automated tools. * Disciplined reporting, tracking, and resolution of product defects. Implementation of reporting technologies, integrated with automated test runs. * Implementation and management of automated test scripts in CI/CD pipelines. * Experience carrying out code reviews and adhering to best practice guidelines. * Clear understanding of defect management lifecycle. * Hands on knowledge of SQL. * Knowledge of scripting languages (Shell, Perl, Python etc.). * Excellent verbal and written communication skills who excels when working in a multi\-disciplinary agile team. **Why you will enjoy working at BearingPoint Ireland?** ------------------------------------------------------- We work in an open and collaborative way, where new ideas are always welcome. BearingPoint is an environment where potential and high performance is recognised and rewarded. **What we offer:** ------------------ You will receive a competitive salary where you are rewarded for both your individual performance and company performance. You will also receive a best\-in\-class benefits package including holidays, private health insurance, pension contributions, gym/club subscription, wellness programmes, mobile phone and much more. In addition, we offer: **Hybrid working –** where each team has autonomy to determine their in\-person and remote working schedule. **Continuous learning –** we understand that it is imperative to invest in our people's development and we provide external training at prestigious universities such as Oxford and Yale **Social events –** our people perform best when they have a good work life balance, monthly events for all to attend **Diversity** – we are committed to having a diverse workforce where each individual feels they belong and can thrive in a work environment with a strong sense of community **Mentoring \& coaching –** every employee has a development manager to help them grow their career with feedback via continuous conversations **Giving Back –** you will have an opportunity to join our many giving back initiatives including our commitment to sustainability. Our Emissions Calculator gathers accurate emissions data and enables our clients to understand their footprint and track progress on emission reduction and pave the way for a better future. **Together, we are more than business.** ---------------------------------------- To see more from our people with their experiences of BearingPoint please visit our careers website www.bearingpoint.com **About BearingPoint** ---------------------- BearingPoint is an independent management and technology consultancy with European roots and a global reach. The company operates in four units: Consulting, Solutions, Business Services, and Ventures. Consulting covers the advisory business; Solutions provides the tools for successful digital transformation, advanced analytics and regulatory requirements; Business Services provides managed services beyond SaaS; Ventures drives the financing and development of start\-ups. BearingPoint's clients include many of the world's leading companies and organizations. The firm has a global consulting network with more than 10,000 people and supports clients in over 75 countries, engaging with them to achieve measurable and sustainable success. BearingPoint is an equal opportunities employer. Please click here to view our privacy policy. **NO AGENCIES** BearingPoint does not accept unsolicited cvs from 3rd party recruitment agencies without a written agreement in place for placements. BearingPoint is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited cvs and no fee will be paid should the candidate be hired by BearingPoint.

title: Tourism & Operations Internship
description: **ABOUT US** Indie Campers, the go\-to campervan Marketplace, is on a mission of making road trips available to everyone. Thanks to its digital approach and customer\-centric orientation, Indie Campers has developed a strong booking experience and high\-quality road trips at affordable prices. With over one million nights rented through our travel platform, we have welcomed more than 300,000 travelers from 169 countries. We offer a comprehensive and expanding array of road trip possibilities: short\-term RV rentals, long\-term campervan subscriptions, and the possibility of buying one of our vehicles available for sale. Rooted in a deep technological focus, our challenges are both exciting and demanding and require top talent and motivation to be successfully pursued. We are growing our team and looking for those interested in pursuing this dream with us and joining an ever\-developing journey. **THE ROLE** We're on the lookout for passionate students or recent graduates who want to dive into the exciting world of RV and campervan rentals and gain hands\-on tourism experience at our **Dublin depot** at **Clonswords Yard Ballyboughal Co Dublin A41F308, Ireland.** As our **Tourism \& Operations Intern**, you'll be the friendly face of our brand \- welcoming guests, setting them up for road trips of a lifetime, and ensuring their journey ends just as wonderfully as it began. If you thrive on meeting new people, love a challenge, and enjoy the buzz of a fast\-paced environment, your next adventure starts here! **WHAT WILL YOU WORK ON?** **Front\-office activities:** * **Customer service and sales:** Take care of check\-ins and check\-outs, register guests, process payments, explain campervan functionalities, terms and conditions, upsell road trip extras. **Back\-office activities:** * **Operations handling:** Align the reservations with the available campervans, verify and prepare campers and road trip extras and kits. * **Quality control:** Maintain the campervans in top condition, identify and act on possible maintenance or repair needs. * **Support activities:** Support the team in monitoring the existing stock, coordinating the fleet of campervans, troubleshooting operational issues. **WHO ARE WE LOOKING FOR?** * You are a student or recent graduate in Hospitality, Tourism, International Studies, Business Administration or a related field looking for an internship; * You are fluent in English, have a work permit (if applicable), and avalid driver's licence (for both manual and automatic vehicles); * You like international environments and meeting new cultures; * You possess a natural talent for connecting with people and have a strong customer service orientation; * You have a problem\-solving mindset, thriving to create solutions and feeling comfortable with getting your hands dirty. **THE INDIE COMMITMENT** * All internships are compensated; * Online and on\-the\-job training to develop relevant skills to your growth: guest relations, customer service, problem\-solving, communication, team spirit, organization, time management, fleet coordination, supplies management and logistics; * International opportunities and possibility of integration in Indie Campers after the internship. ***Are you ready to Go Indie?***

title: Trade Operations Analyst
description: **Key Responsibilities** * Oversee the processing of trades from execution through to final settlement * Investigate and resolve transaction discrepancies, breaks, and exceptions * Maintain regular communication with internal teams and external parties to ensure timely issue resolution * Monitor daily activity to ensure transactions are completed within expected timelines * Assist in identifying process inefficiencies and contribute to improvements in operational workflows * Support risk and control processes by highlighting potential issues and ensuring adherence to procedures **Requirements** * Relevant experience in financial services or operations (typically 1–3 years) * Understanding of transaction processing, settlements, or post\-trade activities * Strong attention to detail and problem\-solving skills * Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast\-paced environment * Good communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively across teams * Familiarity with industry systems and processes is beneficial but not essential

title: Trainee Accountant
description: **Vacancy Name** Trainee Accountant **Vacancy No** VN1927 **Employment Type** Fixed Term **Duration** N/A **Location City** Dublin **Location Country** Ireland **Company Description** Xeinadin was established in 2019 when a number of leading business advisory and accountancy practices across the UK and Ireland came together to re\-imagine the future of accountancy. Our collective mission to provide locally forged, trusted business advice to SMEs through forward\-thinking, close\-knit relationships remains pivotal to our growth. It's our people who drive our business forward, and we offer them future\-focused career opportunities whilst supporting individual specialisms. Our regional offices of over 3000 colleagues operate collaboratively, combining collective expertise to maximise potential. **Description** We are currently recruiting Trainee Accountants for our to join our growing, high performing team, with great opportunities for progression, skills and knowledge. The successful candidate will gain experience across all departments and get exceptional training and support. They will engage with clients from early stages and benefit from an exceptional study leave and remuneration package. **Key Responsibilities** Preparing financial statements; Participating in the performance of audits; Compiling management accounts and other management reporting and bookkeeping; Preparing corporation Tax, VAT, RCT and Income Tax returns; Preparing and processing Payroll; Liaising with banks, legal firms, Revenue and other professional advisers; Managing client Company Secretarial requirements; Work under the supervision of senior staff members and provide support. **Key Requirements** Expect to achieve at least a 2\.1 in accountancy or a business related degree; Have obtained 420\+ points at Leaving Cert level (or equivalent) Be motivated and able to work both as part of a team and independently; Possess strong communication and English language skills; Be enthusiastic about both developing their career Endeavour to continually improve their skills and knowledge. **Additional Requirements** . **Model** Office Based **Salary** Competitive **Benefits** Benefits - Company Pension Scheme - 25 days of annual leave \+ bank holidays - Additional annual leave days from certain levels of seniority - Ability to buy up to 5 days of annual leave to reach a maximum of 30 days per annum - Business closure over Christmas\* - Life Assurance x4 annual salary - Enhanced family leave policies - Enhanced Company Sick Pay - Employee Assistance Programme – 24/7 support, free and confidential - Corporate Discounts Platform Flexible Benefits platform with ability to opt\-in to various insurances (level of seniority dependent \& self\-funded at corporate rates) such as: - PMI single or family - Critical Illness Cover - Cash plan - Cycle to work - Eye care - Dental - subject to exceptions and business needs

title: Trainee Animators
description: Kavaleer Productions are currently hiring for Trainee Animators to join their team. **About the Role:** ------------------- This is a fixed term contract and ONLY open to applicants ***living in the Republic of Ireland***. **Teams starting from:** May/June 2026 Applicants under consideration will be required to complete an animation test before final selection. The function of the Animation Team is to work with the Animation Director and Assistant Animation Director to ensure the highest level possible of animation throughout the production under the direction of the Offshore Director. ### **Ideal Candidate:** * Demonstrated knowledge of the principles of 2D animation * A keen interest in learning more about the production process * ***Experience in Adobe Animate is essential\**** * Strong and clear communication skills * Ability to work within a fast\-paced series production schedule * Excellent organisational and management skills * Ability to adapt to changes within a highly dynamic environment * Strong attention to detail is critical #### ***Closing date for applications is Wednesday, 15th April.*** *For more information or to apply for this role, please email* ***jobs@kavaleer.com****, attach a CV and showreel and state* ***‘Trainee Animator’*** *in the subject line.* **About Kavaleer:** ------------------- Kavaleer Productions was founded in 2001 and over the past 20 years have forged a position as one of Ireland’s leading animation studios, having produced over a dozen TV shows for broadcasters such as BBC, Sky, Netflix and Nickelodeon. title: Trainee Financial Advisor
description: **Job Description:** Job Title: Financial Advisor (Qualified or Trainee) Company: Choice Financial Location: Co. Kildare (In\-Office Role) Salary: €30,000 – €40,000 (DOE) \+ Uncapped Commission \| O.T.E. €70,000\+ About Us Choice Financial is a fast\-growing, client\-first financial services firm based in Co. Kildare. We specialise in delivering personalised protection, pension, and investment solutions. With a strong focus on collaboration, mentorship, and long\-term development, we provide our team with the tools and support to thrive in a regulated and rewarding environment. The Opportunity We are seeking both qualified and trainee Financial Advisors to join our in\-office team. Whether you're new to the industry or already experienced, you'll receive internal training, access to client leads, and full mentoring support as you develop your advisory career. You'll initially focus on protection products, with the potential to expand into pensions and investments upon completing relevant CPD and qualifications. Key Responsibilities * Conduct fact\-finding meetings and assess clients’ needs, goals, and risk profiles * Provide tailored advice on protection products (life cover, income protection, etc.) * Build strong relationships through regular client follow\-up and service excellence * Work with provided leads to grow your client base and meet monthly targets * Stay current with product updates, market trends, and compliance requirements * Contribute to a supportive team culture and participate in regular training and mentoring What We Offer * Competitive base salary (€30,000–€40,000 depending on experience) * **Uncapped commission structure** – realistic O.T.E. €70,000\+ * Access to an employee bonus scheme * Internal training and **support to achieve your full QFA qualification** * Warm leads provided – no cold calling * Ongoing professional development through CPD * Access to an **employee mentoring programme** and collaborative team support * Clear career progression opportunities within the firm What We're Looking For * Prior experience in financial advice is a plus but not required * Relevant sales experience highly desirable * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Self\-motivated with a genuine interest in helping clients achieve financial peace of mind * Willingness to learn and pursue industry qualifications * A team player who thrives in a goal\-driven environment How to Apply If you’re ambitious, client\-focused, and eager to develop your financial advisory career, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now with your CV and a short cover letter explaining what attracts you to this opportunity. Choice Financial is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds and experiences. Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company pension * Flexitime Ability to commute/relocate: * Ballymore Eustace, County Kildare: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Leaving Certificate (required) Experience: * Sales: 1 year (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Location: * Ballymore Eustace, County Kildare (required) * Work Location: In person

title: Trainee Industrial Painter – Manufacturing
description: **Trainee Industrial Painter – Manufacturing** We are recruiting a trainee painter to join our paint shop team. This role is ideal for someone practical, physically fit and interested in learning a skilled trade in manufacturing. No previous experience is required. Attitude and willingness to learn are more important than experience. You will be trained in industrial paint systems used in heavy manufacturing, working alongside experienced team members and with support from a world\-class paint supplier. This is not a quick course. Learning to paint properly takes time. Over approximately three years you will gain solid industrial painting experience, a valuable skill used across engineering, transport and manufacturing industries. This role suits someone who enjoys practical work and wants to learn a skilled trade rather than work in an office. **What the job involves** * Preparing steel equipment for painting, including time working in the blast shop * Assisting with spray painting and finishing * Learning industrial coating systems and application techniques * Maintaining high quality standards in the paint shop **What we are looking for** * Physically fit and comfortable with hands\-on manual work * A good attitude and willingness to learn * Reliable and punctual * Interested in developing a practical trade **What we offer** * Structured on\-the\-job training * Support from experienced team members * Training with a leading international paint supplier * The opportunity to build a long\-term skill in manufacturing **Salary** * Starting rate from €15\.00 per hour during the initial training period * Pay increases on a sliding scale as skills and experience develop * Fully trained industrial painters typically earn €18 – €22 per hour depending on experience Portnahinch, Portarlington, R32CD40 Monday to Friday 8\.30\-5\.00

title: Trainee Sales Consultant
description: **Trainee Sales Consultant** **Experience:** None Required – Full Training Provided Are you a people person" with a drive to succeed but feel stuck because you don't have "industry experience"? At **Blackstone Motors**, we believe that the right attitude is more valuable than a CV full of jargon. We are looking for a motivated, energetic, and charismatic individual to join our award\-winning team in Dundalk as a **Trainee Sales Consultant**. If you have the spark, we have the fuel to help you build a lifelong career. **What’s in it for you?** * **Comprehensive Training:** We don’t just throw you in at the deep end. You’ll receive hands\-on mentoring and professional sales training. * **Career Progression:** We prefer to promote from within. Your growth is our growth. * **Competitive Package:** A solid base salary plus a commission structure that rewards your hard work. * **Company Culture:** Join a fast\-paced, supportive, and professional environment where your contribution is truly valued. **The Role** As a Sales Consultant, you aren’t just "selling cars"—you’re helping people find the right vehicle for their lives. Your day\-to\-day will include: * Greeting customers with a warm, professional welcome. * Learning our vehicle lineup inside and out (Renault, Dacia, and our Premium Used selection). * Guiding customers through the buying journey, from the initial test drive to the final handover. * Utilizing digital tools and social media to showcase our stock. * Ensuring every customer leaves Blackstone Motors with a smile. **What we are looking for:** * **The "Spark":** You are confident, approachable, and love talking to people. * **A Full Driving Licence:** This is essential for the role. No Disqualifications or Endoresement. * **Resilience:** Sales can be challenging, but you’re the type of person who stays positive and keeps going. * **Professionalism:** You take pride in your appearance and your work. * **Tech Savvy:** You’re comfortable using basic computer systems and apps. **How to Apply** Think you have the drive to join the Blackstone team? We want to hear from you! Please send your CV and a brief note telling us why you’re the right fit for our Dundalk branch to **noel@blackstonemotors.ie** Job Type: Full\-time Ability to commute/relocate: * Dundalk, County Louth: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Work Location: In person

title: Trust & Safety New Associate - GTNS - Viacon - Hebrew - CL13
description: **Position:** Trust \& Safety New Associate \- GTNS Viacon CL13 \- Hebrew **Location:** Dublin, Ireland **Contract**: Full\-time 40 hours per week, 12 month contract (with view to extension) **Work Hours**: Must be flexible to work in a 24/7 environment. **Job Description** Accenture is a trusted, innovative, comprehensive, and experienced partner to leading platform companies. The Trust and Safety offering within Accenture Operations helps keep the internet safe and helps platform companies accelerate, scale, and improve their businesses. Content moderators serve as an important line of defense by helping improve the quality and safety of online content while gaining hands\-on job experience. You will deliver value to our clients, while making a larger impact on society. **You will be responsible for analyzing and reviewing user profiles, audio, videos, and text\-based content and/or investigating, escalating and/or resolving issues that are reported by users or flagged by the system.** **Due to the nature of the , the individual may be exposed to flashing lights or contrasting light and dark patterns.** **Content moderation is meaningful work that helps keep the internet safe. It may also be challenging, at times. In the context of this , individuals may be directly or inadvertently exposed to potentially objectionable and sensitive content (e.g., graphic, violent, sexual, or egregious). Therefore, content moderators need strong resilience and coping skills. We care for the health and well\-being of our people and provide the support and resources needed to perform their responsibilities. Active participation in Accenture’s well\-being support program, designed specifically for the Trust \& Safety community, provides valuable skills to promote individual and collective well\-being.** **Details** * Review, classify and/or remove content according to client guidelines, using specific tools and channels * Understand and remain updated on changing client policies and guidelines * Investigate, resolve, and relay complex content issues to the broader Trust and Safety team * Serve as an advocate for the user community * Participate in process improvement initiatives that improve quality and efficiency of work * Participate in continuous training programs and workgroup discussions for optimal development in the * Engage in conversation around socially sensitive topics with the purpose of keeping our communities safe **Basic Qualifications/Skills Needed** * Strong coping, emotional resilience, and stress\-management skills * Excellent comprehension, communication, and Hebrew skills * Ability to perform duties objectively, devoid of inherent biases or personal beliefs, in the best interest of client policies * Strong attention to detail * Comfort synthesizing and analyzing information from multiple streams * Strong critical thinking and decision\-making skills * Deep familiarity and passion for the internet, internet platforms, and internet culture * High affinity and cultural awareness of political and social situations regarding the relevant market and region that will be supported * Ability to work well individually and as part of a team * Ability to work differing rotations/shifts and non\-standard work hours * Flexibility in meeting changing business needs in a fast\-paced environment * Commitment to quality, efficiency, and effectiveness * Previous business process outsourcing, customer service, or content moderation experience is a plus * e.g., over 18 years of age * shift timings/requirements to work weekends and holidays Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity. Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com **About Accenture** Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Combining unmatched experience and specialized skills across more than 40 industries, we offer Strategy and Consulting, Interactive, Technology and Operations services — all powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centres. Our 674,000 people deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity every day, serving clients in more than 120 countries. We embrace the power of change to create value and shared success for our clients, people, shareholders, partners and communities. Visit us at accenture.com.

title: Trust & safety intern
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. In this six\-month internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Enhance user safety.** Monitor emerging threats, abuse patterns, and relevant regulatory developments at scale—ensuring they are effectively reflected in products used by millions. * **Implement our Trust \& Safety framework.** Adapt general best practices to product\-specific risks and needs, collaborating with Product and Engineering teams to embed safety into features and workflows. * **Cooperate with moderation teams.** Ensure clarity on evolving policies and streamline moderation processes, personally handling critical or sensitive escalations whenever necessary. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £41,255 in London and €39,892 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £56,345 and £85,797 in London, and €54,346 and €82,557 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Internship. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: UX Designer
description: **UX Designer (X2 positions \- 1 junior/ 1 senior)** **Hybrid \| Dublin \| 3 days onsite** **€60,000 – €80,000 DOE** **The Opportunity** We’re partnering with a global financial services organisation undergoing a major transformation of its digital product ecosystem. Our client operates in a complex, high\-stakes environment where the products you design support treasury operations, trade processing, and collateral management for institutional clients worldwide. You’ll join a global design group working across multiple enterprise platforms as part of a large programme to **unify branding, interaction patterns, and user journeys** across the organisation’s product suite. **What You’ll Be Doing** You’ll be embedded in cross\-functional product teams, partnering with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders across multiple regions to: * Lead and deliver **end\-to\-end UX work** , from research and discovery through to high\-fidelity prototypes * Map complex user journeys across treasury, trade, and collateral workflows * Translate business and regulatory requirements into intuitive interaction patterns * Contribute to and evolve a shared design system across multiple enterprise applications * Advocate for design decisions with data, insight, and clear rationale * Help bring consistency to a product landscape that is being actively consolidated and modernised Depending on experience, you may also: * Lead research initiatives and mentor more junior designers * Shape design strategy within a core product stream that is seeing significant investment **Key Platforms You’ll Work On** You’ll help design experiences across a suite of enterprise products, including: * **Treasury Platform** – a flagship, client\-facing system handling high\-value payments * **Collateral Platform** – supporting complex asset and risk management workflows * **Trade Operations Hub** – a growing product covering trade affirmation and settlement processes These are not greenfield consumer apps—they are dense, data\-heavy tools where great UX has measurable business impact. **What We’re Looking For** We’re interested in designers who enjoy solving complex problems and can bring structure to ambiguity. **You’ll likely have:** * 3–5\+ years’ experience in UX or product design, ideally within complex or enterprise environments * A portfolio showing your thinking—from research and journey mapping to wireframes and prototypes * Strong understanding of usability, interaction design, and information architecture * Experience working with or contributing to design systems * Confidence collaborating with engineers and explaining design trade\-offs **Nice to Have** * Experience working on financial, trading, or other data\-intensive platforms * Hands\-on user research experience (interviews, testing, analytics) * Experience operating within global or distributed teams

title: Vice President - Portfolio Analytics & Reporting, Private Debt
description: We are global private markets specialists delivering tailored investment solutions, advisory services, and impactful, data driven insights to the world's investors. Leveraging the power of our platform and our peerless intelligence across sectors, strategies, and geographies, we help identify the advantages and the answers our clients need to succeed. **Position Overview** The SPAR Vice President will provide portfolio analytics and reporting services for the Firm's private market / alternative investment clients and related portfolios, with a focus on private debt. The Vice President will work closely with the Department's senior leadership as well as other departments (e.g. Accounting, research, IR, Business Development) where necessary to provide support to the Firm's global investment activities. As a senior stakeholder of the Department, the SPAR Vice President is expected to provide a combination of client management, team development, and strategic leadership. **Essential Job Functions:** * Client Management * Senior Department resource for client relationship management. * Serve as client lead, overseeing all aspects of the client monitoring and reporting cycle. * Final review and sign\-off for core client deliverables. * Ensure key deadlines are established, continuously strived for, and met on time. * Manage, anticipate, and communicate needs for relevant client deliverables. * Lead and effectively delegate resources to ad hoc client projects, where necessary. * Ensure succession plans and institutional memory are in place as needed to ensure uninterrupted client service (client lead backups, documentation of unique processes/procedures, etc.). * Team Development * Responsible for day\-to\-day leadership of assigned team (i.e. Direct Reports). Provide management of client and project responsibilities, as well as coordination and collaboration with other leaders on shared Direct Report resources, where applicable. * Work with Direct Reports, including Associates and other client leads, to help manage day\-today operations of the team. * Actively develop, engage and mentor Direct Reports by offering challenging work, guidance, and increasing scope of responsibilities based on merit. * Provide explanation and reinforcement of Department\-wide communications, initiatives, and training objectives to Direct Reports. * Encourage and promote skill development of team, objectively identifying strengths and weaknesses of individual Direct Reports and improving the effective operations of the team as a whole. * Serve as primary coordinator and key contributor to annual performance evaluations for Direct Reports. Evaluations will require solicitation of feedback from relevant Department teammates and will be formally presented to Direct Reports in conjunction with SPAR senior leadership. * Actively mentor and develop junior members of the Department (outside of their Direct Reports) and participate in team\-building with the broader Department. * As a senior stakeholder of the Department, the Vice President is held to the highest standard of respectful communication, which is instrumental in setting the tone for the rest of the Department team. 3\. Strategic Leadership * Ability to drive positive change and contribute multi\-faceted leadership to the Department through utilization of unique knowledge, skills, and experience. * Offer ideas for improving overall operations, efficiency, and value\-added services for the Department. * Based on depth of experience, possesses (or is expected to possess) resident/domain expertise on subject matters of importance to the Department and broader Firm. * Work with senior management to implement Department\-wide strategic initiatives amongst Direct Reports. * Perform other duties as requested or as responsibilities dictate. **Education and/or Work Experience Requirements:** * Possesses a bachelor's degree (Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business preferred) * Minimum 7 years of relevant experience preferred (ideally within Private Markets) * Working knowledge of financial accounting and demonstrated ability to learn new performance and portfolio reporting systems * Significant experience with Microsoft Excel * Proficiency in English is a must (verbal and written), Proficiency in German is a plus * Professional designation such as CFA or CAIA is a plus **Other Knowledge, Skills and Abilities** * Proven ability to lead projects and/or teams * Proven ability to multi\-task and manage priorities against key deadlines * Proven ability to work with stakeholders across various departments within the organization * Strong verbal and written communication skills * Familiarity with StepStone portfolio accounting/reporting systems * Highly motivated individual with demonstrated initiative, independence and adaptability * Problem solving skills and ability to oversee execution of accurate and high\-quality finished products * Strong analytical skills * Strong work ethic Interest in global financial markets * \#LI\-Hybrid *At StepStone, we believe that our people are our most important asset and crucial to our success. title: Video Editor
description: **About Us** We help our clients because we are industry\-leading on social media, and at the heart of that is the content. We focus on smart, consistent content, strong creative, and clear calls\-to\-action that drive real results. Everything we do is built around helping brands grow, connect with their audience, and stay relevant in a fast\-moving digital space. We’re big on ideas, execution, and keeping things fresh. **The Role** We’re looking for a talented **graduate with Video Editing** experience to join us full\-time at our office in Sandyford. You’ll be editing high\-quality video content across a mix of brands — from social\-first content to more polished high\-production pieces. You’ll be working closely with our creative team to bring ideas to life and make sure everything we put out looks sharp, engaging, and on\-brand. This role isn’t just about cutting clips — we want someone who understands pacing, storytelling, and what actually works on social. Colour grading ( **DaVinci workflows** ) is a key part of the role, so a strong eye for detail and consistency is essential. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Editing video content for social media, campaigns, and client projects * Colour grading footage to a high standard ( **DaVinci experience is a big plus** ) * Adding motion graphics and visual elements to bring content to life * Working closely with the team on concepts and creative direction * Keeping content aligned with each brand’s tone, style, and goals * Managing multiple projects without letting quality slip **What We’re Looking For** * Strong foundation in video editing ( **DaVinci experience is a big plus** ) * Solid colour grading skills * Good eye for detail, pacing, and what looks good on social * Able to manage your time and hit deadlines without stress * Easy to work with — good communication and team mindset * Someone who is happy working in a collaborative, in\-person environment **Bonus:** * Experience working with brands or creating social media content * Understanding of what’s trending and what actually performs online **Salary \& Details** * €30,000 (graduate position) * Full\-time, on\-site in Sandyford

title: Vision Engineer
description: **Vision Engineer \- shape the picture behind world\-class live broadcasts.** At NEP Ireland, we deliver cutting\-edge live productions to audiences across the globe, and as a **Vision Engineer** , you’ll be right at the heart of it. Working across our fleet of IP, UHD and HD broadcast facilities, you’ll play a key role in ensuring every shot, signal and system performs flawlessly. This is an exciting opportunity to develop your engineering career within a highly skilled team, gaining hands\-on experience across a wide range of projects while working with the latest broadcast technology. With support and mentorship from senior engineers, you’ll build the expertise needed to progress into a lead engineering role. If you’re technically curious, thrive in fast\-paced environments, and want to work on some of the most prestigious live productions in the industry, this is your chance to grow and make your mark. **The Role** The role of Vision Engineer is an exciting position within NEP Ireland Engineering, working on a range of NEP projects with our fleet of IP, UHD and HD outside broadcast trucks, fly\-pack systems, and Broadcast Centres. Under the expert guidance and mentorship of our highly experienced Senior Engineering team, you will continue to grow your professional skills and experiences, with the aim of progressing to a Guarantee Vision Engineer, capable of being the lead Vision Engineer on many of our broadcast facilities. You will work with our IP, UHD and HD outside broadcast trucks, fly\-pack systems and Broadcast Centre facilities. You will play a pivotal role as part of a team, utilising our diverse and scalable combinations of traditional, remote, centralised and cloud\-based workflow, capturing, recording, broadcasting and streaming high quality content for our prestigious client base. This will be enabled by NEP broadcast centres, data centres, venue kits, truck and flypack facilities, not just in Ireland but around the world. **Responsibilities** * Be part of a team responsible for vision control operations. * Assist the Guarantee Engineer in the delivery of outside broadcasts, both at our base in Kilcoole and onsite at various locations, domestically and internationally. * Work effectively in a team with other Vision Engineers to deliver external vision rigs on outside broadcasts. * Assist in the build and deployment of fly\-pack systems, under the guidance of a Senior Broadcast Engineer, from a client specification. * Follow up faults and assist in the maintenance of our fleet and facilities. * First\-line fault\-finding and repair of a range of broadcast and AV audio and video equipment; minimising loss of service at times of equipment failure by rapidly identifying and implementing temporary workarounds. * Interpret and implement instructions and requests from clients, producers, directors and other colleagues. **Skills / Qualifications** * Either + HND level (or equivalent) in a relevant technical or scientific discipline; + Vocational qualification in a media technology field; or + Evidence of suitable in work learning and development, complimented with relevant practical and theoretical training. * Previous experience working in outside broadcast facilities as a Trainee Engineer or Engineer would be beneficial. * Base level knowledge of outside broadcast truck / fly pack workflow is preferred. * Knowledge and experience IP systems would be an advantage. * Good fault\-finding skills (desirable). * An awareness of Health \& Safety. * Desire to advance to a senior audio engineering role. * Developing communication and client facing skills. * Good time management skills and ability to work to very tight deadlines and a flexible attitude. * Logical thought processes: ability to work well under pressure. * Ability to use initiative; meticulous attention to detail; pro\-active and self\-motivated. *This position will include travel and evening/weekend working. All applicants must be prepared and able to travel internationally. A full clean driving licence is essential.* *The job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties to be performed by the employee.* *The job description may be altered to reflect the business needs of the Company.*

title: Warehouse & Logistics Specialist
description: Job Description A fantastic opportunity has arisen for a **Warehouse \& logistic specialist.** Reporting to the Associate Director Logistic Management, the primary role of the Warehouse \& Logistics specialists Key Team member to the Logistics team providing support for indirect activities that impact the flow of materials both into and through the Site. Working with both Warehouse team as well as other Site stakeholders and above Site teams to ensure systems and procedures effectively manage safety and Quality risks whilst maximising operational effectiveness. These procedures/ systems include change controls, shipping \& regulatory controls, training \& development, compliance (safety \& quality) audits and continuous improvement initiatives. **What you will do:** * Leading / supporting Compliance Investigations with both Site and external teams * Involved in both safety \& Quality audits (both internal and external) as a key SME. * Facilitate agreed Continuous Improvements and Process improvements within the * Warehouse/Sampling Operations area. This includes agreed Supply Chain \& Site Hoshin initiatives/ NPI etc. * Compliance SME for the department/ technical area for QMS topics, QRAs, change controls etc. * Support Warehouse team to ensure that the workload is correctly level loaded to maximize * efficiency in the medium to long term in the area. * Translate technical knowledge for focus/ SME activity to develop competence in consultation with * Team for improving way in which activities are performed, alignment with compliance (quality, regulatory, safety) expectations; reflected in the procedures \& training competency packages for impacted Team members. * Own agreed Inventory management activities – cycle counts, discards management, SAP training for team. **In order to excel in this role, you will have:** * Level 7 qualification in a related discipline. * Three to five plus years’ experience in a GMP/regulated environment. * Proven record of accomplishments of working in a Pharma/Biopharma company or related industry is desirable. * Excellent interpersonal skills required, ability to interact professionally with all staff, both up and down the organisation * Organisation Skills – Ability to manage multiple priorities and know when to escalate issues for resolution. * Good influencing / collaboration skills and teamwork mindset * Coaching others by sharing your skill set and expertise. * Experience using ERP systems at a manufacturing site. * Supply chain material planning experience is desirable. * Proven ability to using a wide variety of lean tools and building capability in teams you have led that becomes the standard way work gets completed. **Required Skills:** cGMP Regulations, Driving Continuous Improvement, GMP Documentation, Inventory Control Management, Logistics, Logistics Coordination, Procurement, Production Planning, Shipping and Receiving, Space Utilization, Supply Chain Metrics, Warehouse Administration**Preferred Skills:** Current Employees apply HERE Current Contingent Workers apply HERE **Search Firm Representatives Please Read Carefully** Merck \& Co., Inc., Rahway, NJ, USA, also known as Merck Sharp \& Dohme LLC, Rahway, NJ, USA, does not accept unsolicited assistance from search firms for employment opportunities. All CVs / resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at our company without a valid written search agreement in place for this position will be deemed the sole property of our company. title: Warehouse Associate
description: **WHO WE ARE:** EOS IT Solutions is a Global Technology and Logistics company, providing Collaboration and Business IT Support services to some of the world's largest industry leaders, delivering forward\-thinking solutions based on multi\-domain architecture. Customer satisfaction and commitment to superior quality of service are our top business priorities, along with investing in and supporting our partners and employees. We are a true International IT provider and are proud to deliver our services through global simplicity with trusted transparency **THE POSITION** Based at EOS IT Solutions' facility in Swords, the Warehouse Associate is primarily responsible for ensuring that day\-to\-day operational logistics activities run effectively and efficiently. This includes cycle counting, inventory receipting, and processing of all inbound and outbound shipments. The Warehouse Associate will work as part of a dynamic and growing team to deliver EOS suppliers' and customers with an unrivalled service and contribute to our growth and success. **WHAT YOU WILL DO** * Ensuring the day\-to\-day operational Logistics activities run smoothly and efficiently. * Loading and unloading trucks, cycle counting, kitting, picking, packing, shrink wrapping, labelling, and palletizing products. * Support teams with internal moves, additions, and changes. * Receiving and processing all equipment shipments. * Responsible for performing the physical or administrative/physical tasks storing, and distributing of products, materials, parts, supplies and equipment. * Sorting and placing materials or items on racks, shelves, or in bins according to pre\-determined sequence such as size, type, style, colour, or product code. * Examines stocks and distributes materials in inventory. * Performs other duties and responsibilities when needed. **ESSENTIAL CRITERIA:** * Excellent follow through skills, coupled with strong organizational skills and solid communication and interpersonal skills. * Ready, willing, and able to deliver excellence independently or collaboratively. * Basic knowledge of warehouse safety and operations. * Be an analytical thinker who can help organize and complete tasks. * Be innovative and self\-motivated, with a keen ability to ask questions, as well as a self\-directed learner. * Excellent interpersonal skills, including relationship building and collaboration within a diverse, cross\-functional team. * Ability to pay meticulous attention to detail. * Able to lift heavy boxes and equipment. **DESIRABLE CRITERIA:** * Previous experience working in a Warehouse or similar environment * Forklift License \- preferably CB/Aisle Master * Possess experience with inventory management. **BELONGING AT EOS:** *EOS is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We invite you to consider opportunities at EOS regardless of your gender; gender identity; gender reassignment; age; religious or similar philosophical belief; race; national origin; political opinion; sexual orientation; disability; marital or civil partnership status or other non\-merit factor.* \#IND
